title: crepidomanes auriculatum
intro: Crepidomanes auriculatum, also known as the earlobe spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The leaves of Crepidomanes auriculatum contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. Crepidomanes auriculatum can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is sometimes used in traditional herbal remedies to treat various health conditions.
